programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.

programme.title: CBC News: Morning
programme.desc: What do you need to know about the world around you? Heather Hiscox takes the helm as the anchor for this weekday-morning news program to tell you. Joining her is Colleen Jones with weather and sports info., and Danielle Bochove with all you need to know about the business world.

programme.desc: A boy's disappearance leads to a community turning on his parents.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary makes clean-up a breeze by whipping up four one-pot recipes that will leave you wondering, where the heck is the mess?

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary gives a quick crash course in French cuisine with recipes that sound fancy but demystify classic techniques.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary shows how versatile meal planning can be with four recipes that future-you will thank you for.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Leftovers can seem uninspiring, but Mary gives them new life by showing how to use them as ingredients in brand new recipes.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: If cheese was a food group, it would be Mary’s favourite. This week Mary pays homage to fromage with four recipes where cheese is the star.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Not every meal needs to have meat as the star. Mary shows how simple and delicious Meatless Mondays can be.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary's delivering fast food flavors at home with dishes that are simple to make.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary has three foolproof cake recipes to make sure any celebration is a hit.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary makes the freezer a friend by throwing together four recipes with frozen ingredient staples.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary shows how embracing your oven and baking for breakfast is the ultimate game changer to switch up your daily grind.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Host Bianca Osbourne combines the cuisines of Central America and Northern Europe to create inventive dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Host Bianca Osbourne combines the cuisines of Georgia and Hong Kong to create delicious dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Host Bianca Osbourne combines the cuisines of South Korea and Spain to create inventive dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Host Bianca Osbourne celebrates the tastes of Ethiopia and Brazil and creates delicious dishes combining their cuisines.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Host Bianca Osbourne celebrates the tastes of Morocco and Thailand and creates delicious dishes combining their cuisines.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong combines the cuisines of Vietnam and France to create delicious dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong celebrates the tastes of India and the USA and creates delicious dishes combining their cuisines.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong celebrates the tastes of Iceland and Jamaica.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong combines the tastes of Ireland and Japan to create delicious pub food fare.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong combines the tastes of Croatia and Barbados to create delicious dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong celebrates the tastes of Belgium and Madagascar to create delicious dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong celebrates the tastes of Peru and Lebanon to create delicious dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong combines the cuisines of South Africa and Portugal to create inventive dishes.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Host Tyrone Edwards surprises South African born Shameen Miller with the results of her DNA test.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Andrea gets her DNA test results and learns about her heritage's cuisine.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Laird Hercules is surprised with the results of his DNA test.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Host Tyrone Edwards surprises Mary Bosco with the results of her DNA test and explores her newly revealed heritage’s cuisine.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Host Tyrone Edwards surprises Brazil-born Iafa Almeida Cac with her DNA test results.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: After getting the results of her DNA test, PJ Kerr explores her newfound heritage through cooking.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary “healthies up” her favourite comfort foods by incorporating more nutrients, and finding ways to amp up the health factor.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Join Mary Berg as she takes it step by step through recipes guaranteed to impress without the stress.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Ingredients packed in cans and jars can be just as good as fresh ones.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary amps up the cravings, and savings, with recipes that prove you don't have to spend a fortune to make delicious dishes.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary will have you saying Grazie! with four deliziosa pasta recipes that keep it simple but amp up the flavour.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Pie making can be daunting, but Mary provides a pastry crash course with three different types of dough.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Whether you’re working from home or at the office, Mary’s recipes mean you don’t have to sacrifice a tasty lunch for convenience.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary shows you how to cater to food intolerances and allergies by amping up the flavour instead of using blah ingredient substitutions.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary takes a spice-fueled adventure around the world and helps you navigate the grocery store spice aisle.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Whether you’re cooking for finicky friends or tricky tots, Mary’s got it covered with strategies to feed even the pickiest eaters.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary recommends a retro theme for impressing guests, and these old-school recipes with a special Mary twist.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Allow Mary to bring your kitchen outside with delicious recipes that will demystify the grill and keep your eyebrows intact.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: A lemon raspberry loaf with a bright pink glaze, lemon Parmesan chicken.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Prepare your party platters! Mary’s handy hors d’oeuvres elevate classic recipes while keeping it simple.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary is all about cooking smarter, not harder. She shares no-sweat supermarket shortcuts to save you from kitchen time and stress.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Save your abundance of fresh herbs from wilting away with Mary’s recipes to use ‘em up in a jiff.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Celebrate the season with Mary’s recipes that take advantage of limited-edition produce, including tomatoes, zucchini, peppers and berries.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Whether you’re a newbie baker or a pro who wants to take it easy, these recipes are all about supermarket hacks to raise your baking game.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary “healthies up” her favourite comfort foods by incorporating more nutrients, and finding ways to amp up the health factor.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Join Mary Berg as she takes it step by step through recipes guaranteed to impress without the stress.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Ingredients packed in cans and jars can be just as good as fresh ones.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary amps up the cravings, and savings, with recipes that prove you don't have to spend a fortune to make delicious dishes.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary will have you saying Grazie! with four deliziosa pasta recipes that keep it simple but amp up the flavour.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Pie making can be daunting, but Mary provides a pastry crash course with three different types of dough.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Whether you’re working from home or at the office, Mary’s recipes mean you don’t have to sacrifice a tasty lunch for convenience.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary shows you how to cater to food intolerances and allergies by amping up the flavour instead of using blah ingredient substitutions.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary takes a spice-fueled adventure around the world and helps you navigate the grocery store spice aisle.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Whether you’re cooking for finicky friends or tricky tots, Mary’s got it covered with strategies to feed even the pickiest eaters.

programme.title: Hubble's Enduring Legacy
programme.desc: The Hubble's iconic images are brought to life to answer the most important questions now facing astronomers.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: Zero Gravity: Life on the International Space Station
programme.desc: ESA and NASA astronauts document six months exploring outer space at the International Space Station.

programme.title: The Age of Hubble
programme.desc: The Hubble Space Telescope has delivered unprecedented discoveries about the nature of our Universe, and more.

programme.title: Search for the Cosmic Dawn: The James Webb Space Telescope
programme.desc: The James Webb Space telescope: on the hunt for the events that gave the universe its start

programme.title: The Next Great Extinction Event
programme.desc: There have been five major extinction events in our planet's history, are we about to experience the 6th?

programme.title: Atlas of a Changing Earth
programme.desc: This documentary reveals the processes melting glaciers, and explores how rising seas may threaten coastlines.

programme.title: Earth in 1000 years
programme.desc: Earth's stores of ice on Greenland and Antarctica are melting, and sea levels rising. Where is Earth headed?

programme.title: Voyage of Curiosity
programme.desc: Curiosity's robot rover exploring Mars is now known as one of the greatest achievements in space exploration.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Ride along on dramatic balloon flights into colorful geomagnetic storms to help revolutionize space travel.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Cosmologists were stunned to learn the universe is flying apart in all directions at an ever-increasing rate.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Did Mars ever develop life? The search for answers reveals the forces that long ago doomed the Red Planet.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Examine the Universe's first micro-moments to learn why the Universe is vast almost beyond our comprehension.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: There are plans to send spacecraft to the Moon to find resources that may one day allow humans to live there.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: What are scientists learning about the history of the Moon and its relationship to the development of Earth?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Massive solar storms attack Earth by disrupting radio signals and navigation systems and harming power grids.

programme.title: Order and Disorder
programme.desc: Jim investigates a strange set of laws that link together everything from engines to humans to stars.

programme.title: Order and Disorder
programme.desc: Jim learns that information isn't just about human communication; it's woven into the very fabric of reality.

programme.title: China's Long March Into Space
programme.desc: Three men sit atop 500 tons of liquid fuel in China's Shenzhou 7 ship, ready to blast 185 miles into space.

programme.title: Moon Shots
programme.desc: With rare archive footage, this stunning one-hour special brings scientific history to life.

programme.title: Hubble's Enduring Legacy
programme.desc: The Hubble's iconic images are brought to life to answer the most important questions now facing astronomers.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: Zero Gravity: Life on the International Space Station
programme.desc: ESA and NASA astronauts document six months exploring outer space at the International Space Station.

programme.title: The Age of Hubble
programme.desc: The Hubble Space Telescope has delivered unprecedented discoveries about the nature of our Universe, and more.

programme.title: Wonders of Northern Europe
programme.desc: Sápmi becomes coastal along Norway, where it splinters off into thousands of islets.

programme.title: Elephant Refugees
programme.desc: When Botswana became poacher-free, a mass migration of elephants followed. It now is a haven for elephants.

programme.title: Rubber Jellyfish
programme.desc: Balloons' harmful effects, to us and to our environment, have been greenwashed and kept a secret for decades.

programme.title: Freaks of the Sea
programme.desc: In the oceans of our planet lurk creatures weirder than our wildest dreams and our scariest nightmares.

programme.title: Global Whale Shark Mystery
programme.desc: Despite its size, the greatest shark in the planet’s oceans, the whale shark, remains an enigma to scientists.

programme.title: City Slickers: Penguin Invasion
programme.desc: Boulders is a popular beach resort in South Africa, where penguins and people compete for habitat.

programme.title: Wonders of Northern Europe
programme.desc: Finland is a country of geographic and cultural transition; at the same time with a very distinct identity.

programme.title: Wonders of Northern Europe
programme.desc: Iceland is a volcanic island with a magnificent environment. Why did humans decide to colonize Iceland?

programme.title: Wonders of Northern Europe
programme.desc: The Norwegian landscapes still bear traces of this tough geological history.

programme.title: Wonders of Northern Europe
programme.desc: Sápmi becomes coastal along Norway, where it splinters off into thousands of islets.

programme.title: Elephant Refugees
programme.desc: When Botswana became poacher-free, a mass migration of elephants followed. It now is a haven for elephants.

programme.title: Rubber Jellyfish
programme.desc: Balloons' harmful effects, to us and to our environment, have been greenwashed and kept a secret for decades.

programme.title: Freaks of the Sea
programme.desc: In the oceans of our planet lurk creatures weirder than our wildest dreams and our scariest nightmares.

programme.title: Global Whale Shark Mystery
programme.desc: Despite its size, the greatest shark in the planet’s oceans, the whale shark, remains an enigma to scientists.

programme.title: City Slickers: Penguin Invasion
programme.desc: Boulders is a popular beach resort in South Africa, where penguins and people compete for habitat.

programme.title: The Wolf: Marine Mammal
programme.desc: British Columbian wolves are mastering fishing and swimming. Will they eventually evolve into marine mammals?

programme.title: The Lost Island of Madre de Dios
programme.desc: Watch divers, geologists, and biologists face extreme conditions to study this unexplored Antarctic land.

programme.title: Shark Cinema Science
programme.desc: This underseas journey explores advanced shark research worldwide with acclaimed filmmaker Andy Casagrande.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: Every year, a master shepherd takes 200 black-headed sheep through the middle of the German capital of Berlin.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: In Kyrgyzstan, herders drive their herds into the mountains to pastures at nearly 2,500 meters (8,000 feet).

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: In northeastern Uganda, a herder finds that a disease affecting cattle threatens his livelihood.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: A Raika camel herd has scattered throughout the dry forest of the Rajasthan land in northwest India.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.sub-title: Living with Alpacas in the Peruvian Andes
programme.desc: The rapidly changing climate threatens the way of life for an alpaca herder in the high Andes of Peru.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: The Kalahari: a thirsty land where brief rains spark vibrant life, soon reclaimed by the desert.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: Africa’s southern coral reefs are vibrant and dynamic worlds in which remarkable creatures that live.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife filmmakers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. A cunning grizzly grabs a salmon off Andreas Kieling's fishing rod, in the depths of the Amazon River a diving team has a spooky encounter with piranhas, in the Mediterranean Sea the currents nearly push Thomas Behrend into the mouths of angry sperm whales defending a newborn - and cameramen who want to film jaguars in the dense jungle of South America become easy prey for the well camouflaged cats. The life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces and failing technical equipment: In the swamps of North Australia violent storms hold up the crossing of tearing rivers, a volcano in the South Seas spits burning lumps of lava into the base camp, and in Siberia a camera team breaks through the ice and has to camp at minus 30°C in the middle of nowhere. The second episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife-filmmakers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife filmmakers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. In the Kruger National Park Josef Jozefowicz steps into a lionesses' den to film her cubs, in Alaska Andreas Kieling mingles with giant elk bulls in order to film them in action, and in Florida his colleague Zoltan Török gets very close to an alligator mother guarding its nest. The life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces, and failing technical equipment: the strong and tearing current of the Yukon River throws Andreas Kieling's boat onto a gravel bank, in the Democratic Republik of Congo camerawoman Heike Grebe is separated from her colleagues in the dense rainforest, in Kamchatka a film team only just escapes a gigantic landslide, and in Finland a hot-air balloon catches fire and becomes the nightmare of Oliver Goetzl and Ivo Nörenberg. The last episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife film-makers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: The Cape of Wild Animals, Episode 5
programme.desc: The movie focuses on committed animal rights activists, who fight for the lives of their charges every day, in diverse protection projects and rescue centres along the South African Garden Route.

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: Baby animals beguile with their innocent charm. But while some remain harmless, others have a deep-rooted instinct. It may take weeks or months of training to hone their skills. Their landscapes and lifestyles maybe very different, but these babies are born to hunt.

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: In a land of dangers, plant-eating animals are always at risk, and never more so than at the time of their birth; out on the open they are vulnerable and there are few places to hide. But luckily some are born to run …

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: It's a water world and life couldn't survive without it. And thanks to it, a mind blowing diversity of creatures thrive beneath the surface. Born to enjoy the riches underwater they must master their body's adaptations, learn to find food, to escape danger and above all become expert swimmers! Whether it's in rivers, on beaches or in the deep ocean, all must overcome the obstacles and fulfil their destiny, all are born to swim! Of all the habitats on earth, the ocean is perhaps the most challenging. Here salt, temperatures, currents and predators can make life difficult; it's not an obvious place to want to bring up your babies… But even in these waters generation after generation beats the odds. They have overcome the challenges and become some of the most beautiful and graceful of creatures. But any baby born to swim has a lot to learn… All over the world there are creatures born to swim, and though humans are not, we seem determined to join them. Perhaps we are envious of their grace. Maybe we are all water babies at heart

programme.title: White Lions: Born Wild
programme.sub-title: White Lions: Born Wild - Episode 1: Against all Odds
programme.desc: The two sisters are born as white as snow in May 2009 in South Africa’s Kruger Park. Growing up in this vast, wild wilderness they must not only overcome the same survival challenges that all young lion cubs face, they must also overcome the threats their high visibility brings. In the African bush, anything unusual stands out and is often eliminated by eagle-eyed predators. Will our cubs survive? The odds are stacked against them – 80 percent of all newborn lion cubs don’t make it to adulthood.

programme.title: White Lions: Born Wild
programme.sub-title: White Lions: Born Wild - Episode 2: Fight for Survival
programme.desc: ‘White Lions – Born Wild’ is a true wildlife story, with strong characters and closely studied behaviour. We follow the cubs’ progress as they embark on the pathway to adulthood, learning to hunt, learning to avoid danger, learning to live in their white coats. This is a strong behaviour-led film, a coming-of-age story of a very rare and unusual pair of lions. Through their eyes, we learn what it is really like to be a lion in Africa.

programme.title: Wildlife Diaries Australia
programme.sub-title: Of Flying Foxes and Heat Waves
programme.desc: While the Tolga Bat Hospital is looking after endangered flying foxes, researchers are using thermographic cameras to search for flying foxes and koalas. But they can also detect bush and wildfire more quickly.

programme.title: Wildlife Diaries Australia
programme.sub-title: Of Sea Turtles and Coral Reefs
programme.desc: The Cairns Turtle Rehabilitation Center (CTRC) raises and cares for sick, or injured turtles, while the Schmidt Ocean Institute goes on a diving mission with a high-tech underwater rover. In this way, we learn firsthand about the state of Australia's sensitive marine animals' natural habitat and what needs to be done to ensure their survival.

programme.title: Wildlife Diaries Australia
programme.desc: Tasmania's Bonorong Wildlife Sanctuary used to be a traditional wildlife park - now it is an innovative and active wildlife sanctuary and home to the famous Tasmanian Devil, to wombats, and many other native species. But unfortunately, "Tassie Devils" are particularly endangered by the Devil Facial Tumor Disease (DFTD). We accompany the team of young, ambitious scientists on their fight against the disease.

programme.title: Wildlife Diaries Australia
programme.desc: Lone Pine Koala Sanctuary was opened in 1927 at a time when Koalas were still being killed on a large scale for the fur trade. Today, hardly anything threatens the natural habitats of these animals as much as the desertification of the forests. We let the drones fly and are there when a new tree is planted.

programme.title: Wildlife Diaries Australia
programme.desc: Visiting the fenced nature reserve Arid Recovery in southern Australia, we witness the care of an injured kangaroo and follow researchers at the Hidden Vale Wildlife Centre near Brisbane as they train native animals to detect tracks of threatening predators and then move to safety.

programme.title: Wildlife Diaries Australia
programme.sub-title: Of Fairy Penguins and GPS Trackers
programme.desc: Australia's largest fur seal colony is located on Phillip Island. Night after night, a breathtaking spectacle takes place here: At sunset, countless fairy penguins return from their hunt for fish. In the station near the penguin colony, pioneering scientific research is conducted around the cute animals. We learn how dedicated scientists are trying to secure the future of the fairy penguins with the help of GPS trackers.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife film-makers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. In Alaska a cunning grizzly threatens the son of Andreas Kieling, on the remote Wrangel Island in northern-east Siberia his colleague Uwe Anders is taken by surprise by a a polar bear, and off the coast of South Africa a diving-team is unexpectedly surrounded by white sharks. But the life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces, life-threatening diseases and failing technical equipment: Deep in the vast rainforests of West Africa cameraman Klaus Scheurich nearly dies of malaria, and in South India a film team narrowly escapes death in a hot-air balloon accident. But they also meet remarkable people such as a zoo keeper in Cameroon who feels at ease playing with half-grown and adult gorillas - the most powerful of all great apes. The first episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife film-makers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife filmmakers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. A cunning grizzly grabs a salmon off Andreas Kieling's fishing rod, in the depths of the Amazon River a diving team has a spooky encounter with piranhas, in the Mediterranean Sea the currents nearly push Thomas Behrend into the mouths of angry sperm whales defending a newborn - and cameramen who want to film jaguars in the dense jungle of South America become easy prey for the well camouflaged cats. The life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces and failing technical equipment: In the swamps of North Australia violent storms hold up the crossing of tearing rivers, a volcano in the South Seas spits burning lumps of lava into the base camp, and in Siberia a camera team breaks through the ice and has to camp at minus 30°C in the middle of nowhere. The second episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife-filmmakers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife filmmakers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. In the Kruger National Park Josef Jozefowicz steps into a lionesses' den to film her cubs, in Alaska Andreas Kieling mingles with giant elk bulls in order to film them in action, and in Florida his colleague Zoltan Török gets very close to an alligator mother guarding its nest. The life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces, and failing technical equipment: the strong and tearing current of the Yukon River throws Andreas Kieling's boat onto a gravel bank, in the Democratic Republik of Congo camerawoman Heike Grebe is separated from her colleagues in the dense rainforest, in Kamchatka a film team only just escapes a gigantic landslide, and in Finland a hot-air balloon catches fire and becomes the nightmare of Oliver Goetzl and Ivo Nörenberg. The last episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife film-makers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: The Cape of Wild Animals, Episode 5
programme.desc: The movie focuses on committed animal rights activists, who fight for the lives of their charges every day, in diverse protection projects and rescue centres along the South African Garden Route.

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: Baby animals beguile with their innocent charm. But while some remain harmless, others have a deep-rooted instinct. It may take weeks or months of training to hone their skills. Their landscapes and lifestyles maybe very different, but these babies are born to hunt.

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: In a land of dangers, plant-eating animals are always at risk, and never more so than at the time of their birth; out on the open they are vulnerable and there are few places to hide. But luckily some are born to run …

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: It's a water world and life couldn't survive without it. And thanks to it, a mind blowing diversity of creatures thrive beneath the surface. Born to enjoy the riches underwater they must master their body's adaptations, learn to find food, to escape danger and above all become expert swimmers! Whether it's in rivers, on beaches or in the deep ocean, all must overcome the obstacles and fulfil their destiny, all are born to swim! Of all the habitats on earth, the ocean is perhaps the most challenging. Here salt, temperatures, currents and predators can make life difficult; it's not an obvious place to want to bring up your babies… But even in these waters generation after generation beats the odds. They have overcome the challenges and become some of the most beautiful and graceful of creatures. But any baby born to swim has a lot to learn… All over the world there are creatures born to swim, and though humans are not, we seem determined to join them. Perhaps we are envious of their grace. Maybe we are all water babies at heart

programme.title: White Lions: Born Wild
programme.sub-title: White Lions: Born Wild - Episode 1: Against all Odds
programme.desc: The two sisters are born as white as snow in May 2009 in South Africa’s Kruger Park. Growing up in this vast, wild wilderness they must not only overcome the same survival challenges that all young lion cubs face, they must also overcome the threats their high visibility brings. In the African bush, anything unusual stands out and is often eliminated by eagle-eyed predators. Will our cubs survive? The odds are stacked against them – 80 percent of all newborn lion cubs don’t make it to adulthood.

programme.title: White Lions: Born Wild
programme.sub-title: White Lions: Born Wild - Episode 2: Fight for Survival
programme.desc: ‘White Lions – Born Wild’ is a true wildlife story, with strong characters and closely studied behaviour. We follow the cubs’ progress as they embark on the pathway to adulthood, learning to hunt, learning to avoid danger, learning to live in their white coats. This is a strong behaviour-led film, a coming-of-age story of a very rare and unusual pair of lions. Through their eyes, we learn what it is really like to be a lion in Africa.

programme.title: Wildlife Diaries Australia
programme.sub-title: Of Flying Foxes and Heat Waves
programme.desc: While the Tolga Bat Hospital is looking after endangered flying foxes, researchers are using thermographic cameras to search for flying foxes and koalas. But they can also detect bush and wildfire more quickly.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.desc: Pavel Hubeny, whose life would be unthinkable without the forest, is the head of the Šumava National Park. He is committed to ensuring that a primeval forest can grow for future generations, in which man does not interfere. This vision of a primeval forest is also shared by the forester Claus Bässler, who wants to counteract the extinction of species by having trees felled to create artificial deadwood and thus a new habitat for rare beetles and fungi. On the Vltava River lies the small town of Krumlov, the "Pearl of the Bohemian Forest" with its Baroque theater and fully preserved stage technology from the 17th century. Here the director Zuzana Vrbová enchants people from all over the world with her productions. Apart from agriculture, it was mainly the abundance of forests that ensured the livelihood of the inhabitants of the Bohemian Forest - including that of the carpenter Philipp von Manz, who processed local wood for his customers all over Germany. Veterinarian Judith Gollner, who grew up in the Austrian part of the Bohemian Forest, lived in Vienna for a long time and made a conscious decision to return. She likes the people and is proud of "her farmers' wives" who share her love of the Bohemian Forest.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.sub-title: The Psychogramm of a Spiritual Landscape
programme.desc: The Bohemian Forest is the source of the Vltava River, home to primeval forests, untouched high moors and bubbling torrents. This archaic stretch of land, which includes the Bavarian Forest, was long considered impenetrable and mysterious. Since the fall of the Iron Curtain, Bavarian border residents have rediscovered new references to this unusual forest landscape for themselves. This is also the case with photo artist Bastian Kalous, who has been documenting his homeland for years with nostalgic-looking Polaroid photographs and is dealing with its eventful history for an illustrated book. Or Elisa Belotti and Marco Heurich, who have dedicated their lives to the lynxes. In order for Europe's largest wild cat to survive in the Bohemian Forest, the lynx researchers fight for every single animal. Iryna Rudenko came to Zwiesel from Ukraine out of a passion for glass as a material to study at the glass school. After graduation, she would like to stay in Bavaria, Anna Hones grew up in Horská Kvilda, the smallest and highest village in the Czech Republic. The village lies just under 1100 meters above sea level, directly on the former border. The farmer, who experienced communism, the fall of the Iron Curtain and the subsequent changes, is now trying to find ways of surviving in this barren region with her children.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The Parque Nacional Peneda-Gerês is the only national park in Portugal. An area of almost 700 square kilometers, located in the extreme north of Portugal. Barren and rocky ridges in the plateaus, contrast with large forests and lush ground vegetation in deep valleys, crossed by lakes and rivers. The park is known for its oak forests, which are predominantly pedunculate and Pyrenean oaks. Among the typical animals of the park are the small Garrano ponies, a very old breed of horse and the only one in Europe that still lives in the wild.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: The archer fish uses a blowgun to capture insects, while the jawfish defends itself by throwing boulders.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Cuttlefish and squid use black fluid to hide from predators, while skunks also use scent glands for defense.

programme.title: Conversations With Dolphins
programme.desc: Dolphins have fascinated humans for centuries, sparking curiosity about the bond between humans and dolphins.

programme.title: Conversations With Dolphins
programme.desc: Scientists are shifting focus to ""how intelligent are dolphins?"" using research from top experts.

programme.title: Beary Tales
programme.desc: A man rescues and adopts two bear cubs, but as they grow older he faces a problem, where to place them.

programme.title: Searching For The Coast Wolves
programme.desc: Shot in the rain forests of Canada's west coast, this documentary records the life of the rare coastal wolves.

programme.title: Hamilton’s Journey - Manatees in a New Light
programme.desc: A manatee has to be extremely unlucky to be given a name. Hamilton is a living example of this.

programme.title: Saving Sea Turtles: Preventing Extinction
programme.desc: Saving Sea Turtles tells the story of how people came together to rescue stranded sea turtles.

programme.title: Giants Of Fortune - The Whale Sharks Of Oslob
programme.desc: Typhoons and overfishing in Oslob, a Filipino village, have severely affected fishermen

programme.title: Magical Reef: The Islands of the Four Kings
programme.desc: Explore Indonesia's underwater paradise, home to vibrant coral reefs and unique sea life species.

programme.title: Animal River Challenge
programme.desc: Rainer Bergmaz travels to the heart of Guyana's Hinterland to find the shy and rare black caiman crocodile.

programme.title: Animal River Challenge
programme.desc: Rainer Bergomaz searches for the Eagle in the canopy of Guyana's unspoiled rainforest.

programme.title: Germany's Underworld
programme.sub-title: From the Alps to the River Rhine
programme.desc: In this episode, we meet powerful hunters like the pike and sinister meat-eating plants like the bladderwort.

programme.title: Germany's Underworld
programme.sub-title: From the Harz Mountain to the North Sea
programme.desc: In this episode meet the ancient Atlantic sturgeon, the ground beetle, the sea eagle, and a family of beavers.

programme.title: Endless Horizon
programme.desc: Namibia offers a chance to see Africa's wildlife, including elephants, lions, and hippos, in its savannahs.

programme.title: Masters Of The Wild
programme.desc: Animals adapt to hostile landscapes using specialized bodies, diets, and behaviors, such as scorpions

programme.title: Megeti
programme.desc: A lone Ethiopian Wolf battles the elements and other predators as she tries to unite with a pack.

programme.title: Bear Whisperer
programme.desc: Follow Bear Whisperer Steve Searles in Mammoth Lakes, California, where bears and tourists collide.

programme.title: The Real Lost World
programme.desc: Follow a team of adventurers on an epic journey to the plateau of Roraima, Venezuela, to The Real Lost World.

programme.title: Addos Elephants
programme.desc: Protected elephants live and thrive in Addo Elephant National Park.

programme.title: Gorilla Doctors
programme.desc: Dr. Mike Cranfield and the gorilla doctors perform check-ups, give medicine to and sometime operate on Africa's endangered mountain gorillas.

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: Find out more about the sensory powers used by animals to make messages loud and clear

programme.title: Animal River Challenge
programme.desc: Rainer Bergmaz travels to the heart of Guyana's Hinterland to find the shy and rare black caiman crocodile.

programme.title: Animal River Challenge
programme.desc: Rainer Bergomaz searches for the Eagle in the canopy of Guyana's unspoiled rainforest.
